summary: Retested backend + frontend for natal wheel interaction regression and chart-to-astrologer context flow. Backend AI astrologer endpoints passed. Desktop wheel interactions (Venus mapping, aspect/house interaction, draft priming, context propagation to /astrologer) passed. Mobile Venus tap still resolves to Ascendant context, causing incorrect quick-ask routing context.

ui_bugs.issue: On mobile viewport, tapping Venus placement hit target resolves to Ascendant context instead of Venus; quick-ask then routes wrong context to /astrologer.

integration_issues.issue: Route transition works, but wrong selected placement context is propagated when clustered hit targets overlap on mobile.

action_items: Fix mobile clustered planet hit-target resolution so direct Venus tap always maps to Venus (not Ascendant).
Re-test clustered planets on mobile after fix for both selected detail card and propagated astrologer context.
Optionally reduce tap target overlap or add nearest-node selection logic/z-index strategy for clustered placements.
critical_code_review_comments: Planet overlay hit areas are rendered in DISPLAY_ORDER with Ascendant last; in clustered regions this can dominate tap resolution on smaller viewports and misroute context.
Desktop behavior is currently correct for Venus mapping and context propagation; issue appears viewport-density/tap-overlap specific on mobile.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded blueprint user credentials from /app/tests/seed_ai_users.json (no new seed created).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Desktop checks passed: Venus click maps correctly, prompt primes dashboard input, aspect and house-sector clicks work, AI response anchored to selected context, and context persists to /astrologer page. Mobile check failed: Venus tap selected Ascendant and propagated wrong context.
rca of the issue: Repro: mobile viewport (390x844) -> login blueprint user -> tap [data-testid='natal-chart-placement-venus'] -> selected detail becomes 'Ascendant in Gemini' -> quick ask opens /astrologer with Ascendant prompt. Likely cause: clustered absolute hit targets overlap, and DOM stacking/order favors another node in dense mobile layout. Mitigation: deterministic nearest-target hit resolution or responsive hit-area resizing/priority handling for clustered nodes.
